root_text.an6.38:1.1: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an6.38:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.38:1.5: “Māhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, evaṁvādiṁ evaṁdiṭṭhiṁ addasaṁ vā assosiṁ vā.
root_text.an6.38:1.6: Kathañhi nāma sayaṁ abhikkamanto, sayaṁ paṭikkamanto evaṁ vakkhati:
root_text.an6.38:2.1: Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, brāhmaṇa, atthi ārabbhadhātū”ti?
root_text.an6.38:2.5: “Yaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, ārabbhadhātuyā sati ārabbhavanto sattā paññāyanti, ayaṁ sattānaṁ attakāro ayaṁ parakāro.
root_text.an6.38:3.1: Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, brāhmaṇa, atthi nikkamadhātu …pe…
root_text.an6.38:3.9: “Yaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, upakkamadhātuyā sati upakkamavanto sattā paññāyanti, ayaṁ sattānaṁ attakāro ayaṁ parakāro.
root_text.an6.38:4.1: Māhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, evaṁvādiṁ evaṁdiṭṭhiṁ addasaṁ vā assosiṁ vā.
root_text.an6.38:4.2: Kathañhi nāma sayaṁ abhikkamanto sayaṁ paṭikkamanto evaṁ vakkhati:

translation_text.an6.38:1.1: Then a certain brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an6.38:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an6.38:1.3: “Worthy Gotama, this is my doctrine and view:
translation_text.an6.38:1.4: One does not act of one’s own volition, nor does one act of another’s volition.”
translation_text.an6.38:1.5: “Brahmin, may I never see or hear of anyone holding such a doctrine or view!
translation_text.an6.38:1.6: How on earth can someone who comes and goes on his own say that
translation_text.an6.38:1.7: one does not act of one’s own volition, nor does one act of another’s volition?
translation_text.an6.38:2.1: What do you think, brahmin, is there an element of initiative?”
translation_text.an6.38:2.3: “Since this is so, do we find sentient beings who initiate activity?”
translation_text.an6.38:2.5: “Since there is an element of initiative, and sentient beings who initiate activity are found, sentient beings act of their own volition or that of another.
translation_text.an6.38:3.1: What do you think, brahmin, is there an element of persistence …
translation_text.an6.38:3.7: “Since this is so, do we find sentient beings who have energy?”
translation_text.an6.38:3.9: “Since there is an element of energy, and sentient beings who have energy are found, sentient beings act of their own volition or that of another.
translation_text.an6.38:4.1: Brahmin, may I never see or hear of anyone holding such a doctrine or view!
translation_text.an6.38:4.2: How on earth can someone who comes and goes on his own say that
translation_text.an6.38:4.3: one does not act of one’s own volition, nor does one act of another’s volition?”
translation_text.an6.38:5.2: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.an6.38:4.1: Māhaṁ, brāhmaṇa → taṁ kiṁ maññasi brāhmaṇa māhaṁ (mr)
